key_changes: Added reasonable restrictions on freedom of speech and expression.
Inserted Article 15(4) for special provisions for socially and educationally backward classes, SCs and STs.
Inserted Articles 31A and 31B and the Ninth Schedule to protect land reform laws.
upsc_trap: First Amendment is linked with Champakam Dorairajan, Article 15(4), Article 31B and Ninth Schedule.

key_changes: Changed the scale of representation in the House of the People.
upsc_trap: It deals with representation in Lok Sabha, not Rajya Sabha.

theme: Trade and commerce of essential commodities
key_changes: Expanded Parliament's power over trade and commerce in certain essential commodities.
upsc_trap: Linked to Concurrent List entry on essential commodities.

key_changes: Restricted judicial review in compensation-related property matters.
Expanded protection for land reform laws.
upsc_trap: Property amendments are repeatedly linked with land reforms and Ninth Schedule.

key_changes: Modified Article 3 procedure regarding time allowed to States to express views on boundary or name changes.
upsc_trap: State consent is not mandatory under Article 3; only views are sought.

key_changes: Gave Parliament power over taxes on inter-State sale or purchase of goods.
upsc_trap: Important for Centre-State taxation before GST.

key_changes: Implemented reorganisation of States on linguistic basis.
Abolished classification of States into Part A, Part B, Part C and Part D.
Provided for Union territories and common High Courts.
upsc_trap: Seventh Amendment is the major constitutional change after States Reorganisation Act, 1956.

key_changes: Extended reservation of seats for SCs, STs and special representation of Anglo-Indians.
upsc_trap: Article 334 is about duration of political reservation, not service reservation.

key_changes: Enabled transfer of certain Indian territory to Pakistan after Berubari reference.
upsc_trap: Cession of Indian territory to a foreign country needs constitutional amendment.

key_changes: Incorporated Dadra and Nagar Haveli as a Union territory.
upsc_trap: Linked to territorial integration after Portuguese colonial rule ended locally.

key_changes: Changed provisions relating to election of Vice-President.
Protected presidential and vice-presidential elections from challenge due to vacancies in electoral college.
upsc_trap: Vacancy in electoral college does not invalidate President or Vice-President election.

key_changes: Incorporated Goa, Daman and Diu as a Union territory.
upsc_trap: Goa became a State later by the 56th Amendment.

key_changes: Inserted Article 371A for special provisions relating to Nagaland.
upsc_trap: Article 371A protects Naga customary law, social practices and land/resources.

key_changes: Incorporated Puducherry as a Union territory.
Enabled legislatures and councils of ministers for certain Union territories.
upsc_trap: Article 239A is different from Article 239AA for Delhi.

key_changes: Raised High Court judges' retirement age from 60 to 62.
Expanded High Court writ jurisdiction in certain respects.
upsc_trap: Supreme Court judges retire at 65; High Court judges retire at 62.

key_changes: Added sovereignty and integrity of India as a ground for restrictions on freedoms.
Modified oath forms to include upholding sovereignty and integrity of India.
upsc_trap: Phrase 'sovereignty and integrity of India' was inserted by the 16th Amendment.

key_changes: Expanded definition of estate under Article 31A.
Added more land reform laws to the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Land reform protection repeatedly used Article 31A, Article 31B and Ninth Schedule.

key_changes: Clarified that the expression State in Article 3 includes Union territories for certain purposes.
upsc_trap: Useful for technical questions on formation of States and Union territories.

key_changes: Abolished election tribunals and enabled High Courts to hear election petitions.
upsc_trap: Election disputes are handled by High Courts under election law, not by the Election Commission.

key_changes: Inserted Article 233A validating certain appointments and judgments of district judges.
upsc_trap: Article 233A is a validation provision.

key_changes: Added Sindhi to the Eighth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Sindhi was added by the 21st Amendment.

key_changes: Inserted Article 244A for formation of an autonomous State within Assam.
upsc_trap: Article 244A is not the same as Sixth Schedule Autonomous District Councils.

key_changes: Extended reservation-related provisions and made changes connected with Nagaland.
upsc_trap: Article 334 is the recurring article for extension of political reservation.

key_changes: Affirmed Parliament's power to amend any part of the Constitution.
Made presidential assent compulsory for constitutional amendment Bills.
upsc_trap: Passed to overcome Golaknath; later read with Kesavananda basic structure doctrine.

key_changes: Replaced compensation with amount in property acquisition context.
Inserted Article 31C to protect laws giving effect to Articles 39(b) and 39(c).
upsc_trap: Original Article 31C protection was limited to Articles 39(b) and 39(c).

key_changes: Abolished privy purses and recognition of rulers of former princely States.
upsc_trap: Privy purses were abolished by the 26th Amendment.

key_changes: Made constitutional changes linked with reorganisation of North-Eastern areas.
upsc_trap: Do not confuse with 13th Amendment for Nagaland or 22nd Amendment for Article 244A.

key_changes: Abolished special privileges of members of former Indian Civil Service.
upsc_trap: Article 314 was omitted.

key_changes: Added Kerala land reform laws to the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: This amendment was directly involved in Kesavananda Bharati.

key_changes: Changed the basis for civil appeals to the Supreme Court from valuation to substantial question of law.
upsc_trap: Civil appeal to Supreme Court is not merely value-based.

key_changes: Increased the maximum elected strength of Lok Sabha.
upsc_trap: Lok Sabha maximum strength questions are linked to Article 81.

key_changes: Inserted Articles 371D and 371E for special provisions relating to Andhra Pradesh.
upsc_trap: Article 371D is now relevant to Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.

key_changes: Required Speaker or Chairman to verify that resignation of a legislator is voluntary and genuine.
upsc_trap: A resignation is not automatically accepted if it is not voluntary or genuine.

key_changes: Added more land reform laws to the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Ninth Schedule entries after 24 April 1973 are subject to basic structure review.

key_changes: Gave Sikkim the status of an associate State of India.
upsc_trap: Sikkim first became an associate State, then a full State by the 36th Amendment.

key_changes: Made Sikkim a full State of India.
Inserted Article 371F for Sikkim.
upsc_trap: Article 371F is special provision for Sikkim.

key_changes: Provided for Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers for Arunachal Pradesh as a Union territory.
upsc_trap: Arunachal Pradesh special State provision later appears in Article 371H.

key_changes: Made satisfaction of President, Governor and Administrators in certain emergency and ordinance matters final.
upsc_trap: Emergency-era amendments often restricted judicial review.

key_changes: Tried to place election disputes of certain high offices beyond judicial review.
Inserted Article 329A.
Added certain laws to the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Article 329A was struck down in Indira Gandhi v Raj Narain.

key_changes: Vested resources of territorial waters, continental shelf and exclusive economic zone in the Union.
Added more laws to Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Article 297 connects marine resources with Union ownership.

key_changes: Raised retirement age of State Public Service Commission members from 60 to 62.
upsc_trap: UPSC and SPSC service conditions are often confused.

key_changes: Added Socialist, Secular and Integrity to the Preamble.
Added Fundamental Duties.
Expanded DPSPs and attempted to reduce judicial review.
Extended Lok Sabha and State Assembly term from five to six years.
Transferred subjects such as education, forests, weights and measures, protection of wild animals and birds from State List to Concurrent List.
upsc_trap: Known as Mini-Constitution; several provisions were reversed by 43rd and 44th Amendments.

key_changes: Restored jurisdiction of Supreme Court and High Courts curtailed by the 42nd Amendment.
Omitted some Emergency-era restrictive provisions.
upsc_trap: 43rd Amendment restored judicial review but 44th is the bigger Emergency-correction amendment.

key_changes: Replaced internal disturbance with armed rebellion for National Emergency.
Made right to property a constitutional legal right under Article 300A.
Restored Lok Sabha and State Assembly term to five years.
Protected Articles 20 and 21 from suspension during Emergency.
upsc_trap: Right to property is not a Fundamental Right after the 44th Amendment.

key_changes: Extended reservation for SCs, STs and Anglo-Indian representation.
upsc_trap: Another Article 334 extension amendment.

key_changes: Expanded meaning of tax on sale or purchase of goods to include works contracts, hire purchase, catering and similar transactions.
upsc_trap: Important for indirect tax evolution before GST.

key_changes: Added more land reform laws to the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Ninth Schedule protection is subject to I.R. Coelho doctrine.

key_changes: Enabled extension of President's Rule in Punjab beyond normal limits.
upsc_trap: Punjab-related President's Rule amendments are low-frequency but factual.

key_changes: Brought Tripura tribal areas under the Sixth Schedule framework.
upsc_trap: Sixth Schedule applies to Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram.

key_changes: Expanded Parliament's power to restrict Fundamental Rights of armed forces, police, intelligence and similar services.
upsc_trap: Article 33 power belongs to Parliament, not ordinary executive authority.

key_changes: Provided reservation for Scheduled Tribes in Lok Sabha and Legislative Assemblies of specified North-Eastern States.
upsc_trap: North-East representation provisions often appear with Articles 330 and 332.

key_changes: Inserted the Tenth Schedule for disqualification on ground of defection.
upsc_trap: Anti-defection law was inserted by the 52nd Amendment; merger exception changed later by the 91st Amendment.

key_changes: Inserted Article 371G for special provisions relating to Mizoram.
upsc_trap: Article 371G protects Mizo customary law and land ownership.

key_changes: Enhanced salaries of Supreme Court and High Court judges.
upsc_trap: Salary provisions are linked to Second Schedule.

key_changes: Inserted Article 371H for Arunachal Pradesh.
upsc_trap: Governor has special responsibility for law and order in Arunachal Pradesh.

key_changes: Made Goa a State.
Inserted Article 371I.
upsc_trap: Article 371I is for Goa.

key_changes: Provided special arrangements for ST reservation in Legislative Assemblies of certain North-Eastern States.
upsc_trap: Article 332 concerns reservation in State Legislative Assemblies.

key_changes: Inserted Article 394A for authoritative Hindi text of the Constitution.
upsc_trap: Article 394A is about authoritative Hindi text, not official language generally.

key_changes: Made special Emergency-related provision for Punjab.
upsc_trap: Article 359A was later omitted.

key_changes: Raised the ceiling on profession tax.
upsc_trap: Profession tax is under Article 276.

key_changes: Reduced voting age from 21 to 18 years for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections.
upsc_trap: Voting age became 18 by the 61st Amendment.

key_changes: Extended reservation for SCs, STs and Anglo-Indian representation.
upsc_trap: Article 334 has multiple periodic extension amendments.

key_changes: Omitted Article 359A and reversed certain Punjab emergency-related provisions.
upsc_trap: 59th inserted Punjab-specific emergency provision; 63rd omitted it.

key_changes: Enabled extension of President's Rule in Punjab.
upsc_trap: Punjab Article 356 amendments are fact-heavy and low-yield.

theme: National Commission for SCs and STs
key_changes: Replaced Special Officer system with National Commission for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
upsc_trap: Separate NCST came later through the 89th Amendment.

key_changes: Added more land reform laws to Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Ninth Schedule does not create absolute immunity.

key_changes: Further enabled extension of President's Rule in Punjab.
upsc_trap: Repeated Punjab-specific extension amendment.

key_changes: Again enabled extension of President's Rule in Punjab.
upsc_trap: Do not confuse 64th, 67th and 68th amendments.

key_changes: Inserted Article 239AA and Article 239AB.
Provided special status, Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers for Delhi.
upsc_trap: Delhi is governed by Article 239AA, not Article 239A.

key_changes: Included elected members of Legislative Assemblies of Delhi and Puducherry in the President's electoral college.
upsc_trap: Only elected MLAs of States plus Delhi and Puducherry participate, not all UTs.

key_changes: Added Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali to the Eighth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali were added by the 71st Amendment.

key_changes: Provided reservation of seats for Scheduled Tribes in Tripura Legislative Assembly.
upsc_trap: Tripura appears in both Sixth Schedule and ST reservation context.

key_changes: Inserted Part IX for Panchayats.
Inserted Eleventh Schedule with 29 subjects.
Provided constitutional status to Panchayats, Gram Sabha, reservation, State Election Commission and State Finance Commission.
upsc_trap: Panchayat powers are not automatically devolved; State law decides actual devolution.

key_changes: Inserted Part IXA for Municipalities.
Inserted Twelfth Schedule with 18 subjects.
Provided constitutional status to urban local bodies.
upsc_trap: Municipalities are Part IXA; Panchayats are Part IX.

key_changes: Provided for tribunals relating to rent, tenancy and landlord-tenant matters.
upsc_trap: Article 323B covers tribunals for multiple specified matters.

key_changes: Placed Tamil Nadu reservation law in the Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Reservation laws in Ninth Schedule may still face basic structure review.

key_changes: Inserted Article 16(4A) enabling reservation in promotion for SCs and STs.
upsc_trap: Article 16(4A) is for promotion reservation for SCs and STs.

key_changes: Added more land reform laws to Ninth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Ninth Schedule questions usually test I.R. Coelho, not list memorisation.

key_changes: Extended SC/ST reservation and Anglo-Indian representation.
upsc_trap: Article 334 extension before later 95th and 104th Amendments.

key_changes: Implemented an alternative scheme of tax sharing between Union and States based on Finance Commission recommendations.
upsc_trap: Important for Finance Commission and divisible pool.

key_changes: Inserted Article 16(4B) allowing backlog reserved vacancies to be treated separately from the 50 percent ceiling for that year.
upsc_trap: Article 16(4B) concerns backlog vacancies.

key_changes: Allowed relaxation in qualifying marks or standards of evaluation for SCs and STs in promotion.
upsc_trap: Article 335 balances SC/ST claims with administrative efficiency.

key_changes: Exempted Arunachal Pradesh from reservation of seats for SCs in Panchayats.
upsc_trap: Because Arunachal Pradesh had no significant SC population.

key_changes: Extended freeze on allocation of Lok Sabha and Assembly seats until after 2026.
Allowed readjustment of territorial constituencies based on 1991 Census without changing number of seats.
upsc_trap: Seat allocation freeze and constituency readjustment are different.

key_changes: Provided consequential seniority in promotion reservation for SCs and STs.
upsc_trap: Article 16(4A) plus consequential seniority is a frequent reservation trap.

key_changes: Inserted Article 21A making education a Fundamental Right for children aged 6 to 14 years.
Modified Article 45 for early childhood care below six years.
Inserted Fundamental Duty under Article 51A(k).
upsc_trap: Article 21A covers 6-14 years; Article 45 covers below six years.

key_changes: Provided for readjustment of constituencies based on 2001 Census instead of 1991 Census.
upsc_trap: Readjustment of constituency boundaries can happen without changing total seats.

key_changes: Inserted Article 268A for service tax before GST.
upsc_trap: Article 268A was later omitted after GST.

key_changes: Separated National Commission for Scheduled Tribes from National Commission for Scheduled Castes.
upsc_trap: NCSC is Article 338; NCST is Article 338A.

key_changes: Made special provision for representation in Assam Legislative Assembly from Bodoland Territorial Areas District.
upsc_trap: Linked with Assam and Bodoland territorial arrangements.

theme: Anti-defection and Council of Ministers size
key_changes: Limited size of Council of Ministers to 15 percent of the House strength, with minimum 12 in States.
Removed split exception from anti-defection law.
Disqualified defectors from ministerial and remunerative political posts.
upsc_trap: Split exception was deleted; merger exception remains.

key_changes: Added Bodo, Dogri, Maithili and Santali to the Eighth Schedule.
upsc_trap: Bodo, Dogri, Maithili and Santali were added by the 92nd Amendment.

key_changes: Inserted Article 15(5) enabling special provisions for backward classes, SCs and STs in educational institutions, including private unaided institutions except minority institutions.
upsc_trap: Minority educational institutions under Article 30 are excluded from Article 15(5).

key_changes: Modified provision for Ministers in charge of tribal welfare in certain States.
upsc_trap: Article 164 has special tribal welfare minister provision.

key_changes: Extended SC/ST reservation and Anglo-Indian representation up to 2020.
upsc_trap: Later, 104th Amendment extended SC/ST reservation but ended Anglo-Indian nomination.

key_changes: Changed the name Oriya to Odia in the Eighth Schedule.
upsc_trap: It renamed Oriya as Odia; it did not add a new language.

key_changes: Added forming co-operative societies to Article 19(1)(c).
Inserted Article 43B as DPSP.
Inserted Part IXB on co-operative societies.
upsc_trap: Supreme Court struck down Part IXB as applied to State co-operatives for lack of State ratification, but it continues for multi-State co-operatives.

key_changes: Inserted Article 371J for special provisions for Hyderabad-Karnataka region of Karnataka.
upsc_trap: Article 371J belongs to Karnataka.

key_changes: Created National Judicial Appointments Commission for judicial appointments.
Inserted Articles 124A, 124B and 124C.
upsc_trap: The 99th Amendment was struck down by the Supreme Court in the NJAC judgment.

key_changes: Gave effect to exchange of territories between India and Bangladesh.
upsc_trap: Land boundary agreement required constitutional amendment because territory was exchanged.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and First Amendment Act, 2016
key_changes: Introduced GST constitutional framework.
Inserted Articles 246A, 269A and 279A.
Created GST Council.
upsc_trap: GST Council recommendations are persuasive, not automatically binding as held in Mohit Minerals.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and Second Amendment Act, 2018
key_changes: Gave constitutional status to National Commission for Backward Classes.
Inserted Article 338B and Article 342A.
upsc_trap: NCBC is Article 338B; SEBC list issue was clarified later by the 105th Amendment.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and Third Amendment Act, 2019
key_changes: Inserted Articles 15(6) and 16(6) for reservation to economically weaker sections.
Enabled up to 10 percent EWS reservation.
upsc_trap: EWS reservation excludes SCs, STs and OBCs already covered by reservation.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and Fourth Amendment Act, 2019
theme: SC/ST reservation extension and Anglo-Indian nomination ended
key_changes: Extended reservation for SCs and STs in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.
Ended Anglo-Indian nominated representation in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.
upsc_trap: SC/ST political reservation continued; Anglo-Indian nomination ceased.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and Fifth Amendment Act, 2021
key_changes: Clarified that States and Union territories can identify socially and educationally backward classes for their own purposes.
upsc_trap: Passed after Maratha reservation-related interpretation of the 102nd Amendment.

short_title: Constitution One Hundred and Sixth Amendment Act, 2023
key_changes: Inserted provisions for reservation of one-third seats for women in Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies and Delhi Legislative Assembly.
Inserted Articles 330A, 332A and 334A.
Reservation will take effect after delimitation following the first census after commencement.
upsc_trap: Women's reservation is not immediately operational; it is linked to census and delimitation.
